Teaching

We are committed to academic excellence and training the next generation of agricultural technology experts. Our teaching portfolio spans undergraduate and graduate-level courses in artificial intelligence, programming, robotics, and digital agriculture.

Master’s Programme: Digital Farming (M.Sc.)

We actively contribute to the Master’s programme in Digital Farming, which focuses on AI, robotics, and digital technologies to solve global challenges in agriculture.

Programme Overview

Focus Areas:

  • Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
  • Agricultural Robotics & Automation
  • Precision Agriculture & Livestock Farming
  • Data Analytics for Agriculture
  • Sustainable Digital Solutions

Key Facts:

  • Language of instruction: English
  • Duration: 4 semesters (120 ECTS)
  • Location: Campus Weihenstephan (near Munich, Bavaria)
  • Start: Winter semester (October)
  • Application deadline: July 15

Admission Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree (≥ 180 ECTS / 6 semesters) in agricultural or technical field
  • English language skills ≥ B2

Further Information:
🔗 www.hswt.de/mdf
✉️ advisory.mdf@hswt.de

Course Offerings

Winter Semester 2025-2026

Foundations of Programming

  • Lectures: Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
  • Exercises: Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi, Harshavardhan Subramanian
  • Introduction to programming concepts and Python for agricultural applications

Grundlagen Python

  • Lectures: Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
  • Fundamentals of Python programming for students beginning their journey in computational agriculture

KI im Agribusiness

  • Lectures: Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
  • Exercises: Dr. Georg Back
  • Applied artificial intelligence techniques for agribusiness applications

Mentoring Programme

  • Instructor: Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
  • Academic and professional guidance for students in the Digital Farming programme

Summer Semester 2026

Agricultural Robotics

  • Co-lecturer: Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi
  • Robotics systems, automation, and intelligent machines for agricultural applications

Artificial Intelligence 1

  • Lectures: Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
  • Exercises: Dr. Georg Back, Harshavardhan Subramanian
  • Foundation course covering machine learning, neural networks, and AI applications in agriculture

Einführung in KI

  • Lectures: Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
  • Introduction to artificial intelligence concepts and methods for beginners

Precision Livestock Farming

  • Co-lecturer: Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi
  • Advanced monitoring systems, sensor technologies, and AI-based decision support for livestock management

Student Projects & Theses

We regularly supervise Bachelor’s and Master’s theses on topics related to our research areas:

  • Computer vision for agricultural applications
  • Machine learning for crop and animal monitoring
  • Time series analysis for smart grids and energy systems
  • AI-based decision support systems
  • Robotics and automation in agriculture

Interested in a thesis project? Contact us at florian.haselbeck@hswt.de to discuss available topics aligned with your interests and our ongoing research.

Teaching Philosophy

Our teaching approach combines:

  • Hands-on learning with real-world datasets and agricultural systems
  • Interdisciplinary perspectives bridging computer science and agriculture
  • Project-based education solving actual farming challenges
  • Industry connections preparing students for careers in agri-tech
  • Research integration exposing students to cutting-edge developments

We believe in empowering students with both theoretical foundations and practical skills to become leaders in digital transformation of agriculture.
